page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-1 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 4.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 ratio of compute resources to memory. The instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or bundled GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options for persistent disk requirements.

Operating with a dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-1 ensures consistent performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. This dedicated allocation makes it a predictable choice for entry-level workloads where performance stability is required. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low resource footprint offers an accessible entry point into the c4a instance family, balancing dedicated compute access with a minimal memory configuration.

This instance is suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, small-scale web serving, development and testing environments, and basic background utility tasks. It is also appropriate for microservices or lightweight containerized workloads that do not require high memory capacity or parallel processing capabilities.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-1 is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 1 dedicated vCPU and 4.0 GB of system memory. Utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio designed for consistent, predictable performance without the resource sharing of burstable configurations. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This resource profile makes the c4a-standard-1 highly cost-efficient for running lightweight, single-threaded applications, development environments, and small-scale web servers. It serves as a stable foundation for microservices and basic background tasks that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of larger multi-core configurations.
